hypothesis: After the accepted stack moved initial entries to a 40% cap and 50% day-2 add-ons, confirmed follow-through winners may be under-allocated when the add-on-only cap remains at 35%. Matching add-on capacity to the current initial cap should release winner-add-on alpha without changing entry, exit, ranking, LLM, or initial sizing.

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where strategy makes money but lags indexes
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with lower win rate

gate4_basis: Rejected despite EV and PnL improving in all three fixed windows because the improvement was too small for Gate 4: aggregate PnL improved only +1.46%, EV did not improve by >10%, Sharpe did not improve by >0.1, max drawdown did not improve by >1 pp, and trade count did not increase.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking remains production-sample limited, so this run tested deterministic add-on capacity using fields already present in production and backtest.

history_guardrails.why_not_simple_repeat: The previous 35% cap experiment was run before the accepted 40% initial cap and 50% add-on stack; the current state creates a new cap mismatch where confirmed winners can be under-allocated relative to initial entries.

risk_assessment.may_hurt: Higher add-on capacity can add concentration to already winning names and may underperform if day-2 follow-through reverses.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Kept addon trigger, fraction, portfolio heat, and initial cap unchanged; fixed-window max drawdown did not expand.

rejection_reason: Positive but immaterial: the small all-window add-on capacity lift does not clear the explicit Gate 4 materiality thresholds, so the code change was rolled back.
next_retry_requires: Do not retry nearby add-on cap tuning at or above 40% without forward concentration evidence or a stronger add-on quality discriminator.
Future add-on work needs a new discriminator such as event/news context or richer lifecycle quality, not another cap/fraction sweep.
